name: Top earners with commission
oracle_sql: SELECT e.first_name, e.last_name, e.salary, NVL(e.commission_pct, 0) AS commission FROM employees e WHERE ROWNUM <= 5 ORDER BY e.salary DESC
pg_sql: SELECT e.first_name, e.last_name, e.salary, COALESCE(e.commission_pct, 0) AS commission FROM employees e ORDER BY e.salary DESC LIMIT 5

name: Department headcount
oracle_sql: SELECT d.department_name, COUNT(*) AS headcount FROM departments d, employees e WHERE d.department_id = e.department_id GROUP BY d.department_name
pg_sql: SELECT d.department_name, COUNT(*) AS headcount FROM departments d JOIN employees e ON d.department_id = e.department_id GROUP BY d.department_name

name: Salary with DECODE
oracle_sql: SELECT first_name, DECODE(department_id, 10, 'Engineering', 20, 'Marketing', 'Other') AS dept FROM employees
pg_sql: SELECT first_name, CASE department_id WHEN 10 THEN 'Engineering' WHEN 20 THEN 'Marketing' ELSE 'Other' END AS dept FROM employees

name: Date formatting
oracle_sql: SELECT first_name, TO_CHAR(hire_date, 'YYYY-MM-DD') AS hired FROM employees WHERE hire_date > TO_DATE('2020-01-01', 'YYYY-MM-DD')
pg_sql: SELECT first_name, TO_CHAR(hire_date::timestamp, 'YYYY-MM-DD') AS hired FROM employees WHERE hire_date > '2020-01-01'::date

name: Oracle outer join syntax
oracle_sql: SELECT e.first_name, d.department_name FROM employees e, departments d WHERE e.department_id = d.department_id(+)
pg_sql: SELECT e.first_name, d.department_name FROM employees e LEFT JOIN departments d ON e.department_id = d.department_id

name: SUBSTR 1-indexed
oracle_sql: SELECT SUBSTR(email, 1, 5) AS prefix FROM employees
pg_sql: SELECT SUBSTRING(email FROM 1 FOR 5) AS prefix FROM employees

name: String concatenation
oracle_sql: SELECT first_name || ' ' || last_name AS full_name FROM employees
pg_sql: SELECT first_name || ' ' || last_name AS full_name FROM employees

name: NVL2 with salary bonus
oracle_sql: SELECT first_name, NVL(commission_pct, 0) * salary AS bonus FROM employees WHERE salary > 80000
pg_sql: SELECT first_name, COALESCE(commission_pct, 0) * salary AS bonus FROM employees WHERE salary > 80000

name: Total orders per employee
oracle_sql: SELECT e.first_name, e.last_name, NVL(SUM(o.amount), 0) AS total_sales FROM employees e, orders o WHERE e.employee_id = o.employee_id(+) GROUP BY e.first_name, e.last_name
pg_sql: SELECT e.first_name, e.last_name, COALESCE(SUM(o.amount), 0) AS total_sales FROM employees e LEFT JOIN orders o ON e.employee_id = o.employee_id GROUP BY e.first_name, e.last_name

name: ROWNUM pagination
oracle_sql: SELECT * FROM (SELECT e.*, ROWNUM rn FROM employees e WHERE ROWNUM <= 10) WHERE rn > 5

name: Hint removal
oracle_sql: SELECT /*+ INDEX(e emp_salary_idx) */ first_name, salary FROM employees e WHERE salary > 90000
pg_sql: SELECT first_name, salary FROM employees e WHERE salary > 90000

name: MERGE statement
oracle_sql: MERGE INTO employees e USING (SELECT 99 AS id, 'New' AS fname FROM DUAL) s ON (e.employee_id = s.id) WHEN MATCHED THEN UPDATE SET first_name = s.fname WHEN NOT MATCHED THEN INSERT (employee_id, first_name) VALUES (s.id, s.fname)
pg_sql: INSERT INTO employees (employee_id, first_name) VALUES (99, 'New') ON CONFLICT (employee_id) DO UPDATE SET first_name = EXCLUDED.first_name

name: Slow query — full table scan (for demo regression)
oracle_sql: SELECT e.first_name, e.last_name, d.department_name, NVL(e.commission_pct, 0) * e.salary AS bonus FROM employees e, departments d WHERE e.department_id = d.department_id AND e.salary > 50000 ORDER BY bonus DESC
pg_sql: SELECT e.first_name, e.last_name, d.department_name, COALESCE(e.commission_pct, 0) * e.salary AS bonus FROM employees e CROSS JOIN departments d WHERE e.department_id = d.department_id AND e.salary > 50000 ORDER BY bonus DESC
